title: Apply Red-Orange Mixed Gradient Theme Across Entire UI + Visible Panel Links Section

requirements.summary: Apply a red and orange mixed gradient theme consistently across the entire frontend UI for both User panel (/) and Admin panel (/admin)
acceptanceCriteria: All primary buttons display a red-to-orange gradient background
Header and navigation bar use red-orange gradient styling
Sidebar (admin panel) uses the red-orange color scheme
Card components, badges, and tabs reflect the red-orange theme
Hover and active states deepen to darker red tones
No blue or purple accent colors remain in either panel
Both User panel (/) and Admin panel (/admin) consistently use the new theme

file_operations.description: Replace existing OKLCH-based color tokens with red-orange gradient theme colors. Update CSS custom properties to use deep reds (#DC2626, #B91C1C) and vibrant oranges (#EA580C, #F97316). Replace primary, accent, and other color variables to reflect the red-orange palette. Ensure hover and active states use darker red tones. Remove any blue or purple color definitions.

Update the Tailwind theme configuration to replace OKLCH-based color tokens with red-orange gradient colors. Define custom colors using deep reds (#DC2626, #B91C1C) and vibrant oranges (#EA580C, #F97316) for primary, accent, and background colors. Configure gradient color stops for from-red-600 to-orange-500 style gradients. Remove blue and purple color definitions from the extended theme.

Update the Header component to use the red-orange gradient theme. Apply gradient backgrounds to the header bar using from-red-600 to-orange-500 or similar red-orange gradients. Update navigation button styles to use the red-orange color scheme. Ensure login/logout buttons display red-to-orange gradient backgrounds. Update hover states to deepen to darker red tones.

Update the Footer component to use the red-orange gradient theme. Apply red-orange gradient backgrounds or borders. Update navigation button styles and social media link icons to use the red-orange color scheme. Ensure consistency with the overall theme.

Update the HomePage hero section and feature highlights to use the red-orange gradient theme. Apply gradient backgrounds to hero sections, feature cards, and call-to-action buttons using from-red-600 to-orange-500 or similar gradients. Update text colors and hover states to align with the red-orange palette. Replace any existing blue or purple accents.

Update the DashboardPage tabs, cards, and buttons to use the red-orange gradient theme. Apply red-orange gradients to tab backgrounds and active states. Update tournament cards, registration cards, and profile cards with red-orange borders and backgrounds. Ensure all buttons (registration, edit, etc.) use red-to-orange gradient backgrounds. Update hover and active states to deepen to darker red tones.

Update the AdminDashboardPage interface to use the red-orange gradient theme. Apply red-orange gradients to the sidebar, tab navigation, and admin panel headers. Update all data tables, cards, badges (pending, approved, rejected, tournament statuses), and buttons (create, update, delete actions) with the red-orange color scheme. Ensure form inputs, modals, and action buttons display red-to-orange gradient backgrounds. Update hover and active states to deepen to darker red tones.

Update the TournamentsPage to use the red-orange gradient theme. Apply red-orange gradients to tournament cards, status badges (upcoming, ongoing, completed, closed), and registration buttons. Ensure card backgrounds, borders, and hover effects use the red-orange palette. Update the registration prompt for unauthenticated users with red-orange styling.

Update the LoginPage to use the red-orange gradient theme. Apply red-orange gradients to the login form container, input borders, and submit buttons. Update step indicators and OTP input fields with red-orange styling. Ensure error messages and validation states use appropriate red tones.

Update the RegisterPage to use the red-orange gradient theme. Apply red-orange gradients to the multi-step registration form, step indicators, input fields, and submit buttons. Update progress indicators and validation feedback with red-orange styling. Ensure consistency with the overall theme.

Update the AdminLoginPage to use the red-orange gradient theme. Apply red-orange gradients to the login form container, input fields, and submit button. Update the admin login branding with red-orange styling to match the overall theme.

Update the TermsPage to use the red-orange gradient theme. Replace the existing colorful cycling styles with red-orange gradient styles for headings, bullets, and paragraphs. Apply red-orange tones to the content container and ensure readability with the new color scheme.

Update the PaymentModal to use the red-orange gradient theme. Apply red-orange gradients to the modal header, input fields, upload button, and submit button. Update progress indicators and validation feedback with red-orange styling. Ensure the modal overlay and borders use the red-orange palette.

requirements.summary: Add a clearly visible 'Panel Links' section on the home page displaying direct clickable links to both User Panel (/) and Admin Panel (/admin)
acceptanceCriteria: HomePage displays a dedicated section with links to both User Panel (/) and Admin Panel (/admin)
Both links are clearly labeled and visually distinct
Section is styled with the red-orange gradient theme
Links are clickable and navigate to the correct routes

file_operations.description: Add a new 'Panel Links' section to the HomePage that displays two prominent, clearly labeled cards or buttons linking to the User Panel at the root URL (/) and the Admin Panel at /admin. Position this section prominently (e.g., below the hero section or as part of the feature highlights). Style both links with the red-orange gradient theme using from-red-600 to-orange-500 or similar gradients. Include recognizable icons (e.g., user icon for User Panel, shield or admin icon for Admin Panel) and descriptive labels such as 'User Panel' and 'Admin Panel'. Ensure both links are clickable and use React Router's Link or navigate to the correct routes. Make the section visually distinct and easy to locate for visitors.
